rationale: Both titles clearly identify the study design (prospective comparative, PFA vs RFA, paroxysmal AF) and the core finding (universal hemolysis with thromboinflammatory effects). Y gains an edge by explicitly naming 'renal consequences' as a third outcome dimension, making the scope of the study more complete and informative for readers deciding relevance. X front-loads thromboinflammatory effects but buries hemolysis mid-title and omits renal consequences entirely, leaving the reader with an incomplete picture of what was measured. Y's structure (universal hemolysis → its consequences) also follows a logical causal chain that aids comprehension, whereas X's ordering (thromboinflammatory effects → hemolysis) slightly obscures the mechanistic hierarchy. Both are acceptably clear, but X's clause arrangement is marginally less fluid.
loser_weakness: X omits the renal consequences dimension, understating the study's scope, and its title ordering places downstream effects (thromboinflammatory) before the proximate cause (hemolysis), weakening the logical flow.
